====ARABIC START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, i, u, b, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, q, r, s, t, w, y, z}.
- Use default CV/CVC syllables; do not spell long vowels as double letters.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "VSO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "binary-past-nonpast",
  "aspect_system": "moderate",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "verbal-affix",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====ARABIC END====

====BULGARIAN START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, v, g, d, z, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, f, h}.
- Overrides default: allows simple consonant clusters in onset and coda (e.g. CCV, VCC), but avoid very long clusters.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "moderate",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "rich",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====BULGARIAN END====

====GERMAN START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, c, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, w}.
- Overrides default: permits dense consonant clusters (CCV, CVCC); long vowels are inferred, not spelled with double letters.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "many",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "moderate",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "inversion"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====GERMAN END====

====GREEK START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, g, d, z, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, f, h, x}.
- Overrides default: allows CV, CVC, and some CCV syllables; no explicit stress or aspiration marking.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "moderate",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "rich",
  "mood_system": "rich",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====GREEK END====

====ENGLISH START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, i, j, k, l, m, n, o, p, q, r, s, t, u, v, w, x, y, z}.
- Overrides default: allows very complex syllables (e.g. CCCV, CVCCC) and irregular spellings.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "isolating",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "none",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "moderate",
  "mood_system": "simple",
  "negation_strategy": "negative-auxiliary",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "inversion"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====ENGLISH END====

====SPANISH START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, c, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, q, r, s, t, v, y}.
- Uses mainly simple CV and CVC syllables; consonant clusters are limited.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "rich",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====SPANISH END====

====FRENCH START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, y, b, c, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, q, r, s, t, v}.
- Overrides default: allows CV, CVC, and some clusters; multiple vowel letters can represent similar vowel qualities.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "rich",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====FRENCH END====

====HINDI START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, c, d, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, v, y}.
- Uses mostly CV and CVC syllables; aspiration/retroflexion are encoded by fixed letter sequences, not new symbols.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SOV",
  "adposition_type": "postpositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-final",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "ergative–absolutive",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "rich",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====HINDI END====

====RUSSIAN START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, y, b, v, g, d, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, f, z}.
- Overrides default: allows moderate clusters in onsets and codas; softness is implied by vowel context and patterns.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "moderate",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "extensive",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "simple",
  "aspect_system": "rich",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====RUSSIAN END====

====SWAHILI START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, w, y}.
- Favors simple CV and CVC syllables; mostly avoids complex consonant clusters.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "agglutinating",
  "affixation_balance": "prefixing",
  "gender_inventory": "many",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-object",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "moderate",
  "mood_system": "simple",
  "negation_strategy": "verbal-affix",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "present",
  "question_marking_strategy": "interrogative-particle"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====SWAHILI END====

====THAI START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, d, f, g, h, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, w, y}.
- Builds words from simple syllable-like groups; tone and vowel length are not marked.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "isolating",
  "affixation_balance": "prefixing",
  "gender_inventory": "none",
  "number_marking": "optional",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "gap",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "no-agreement",
  "tense_system": "none",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "simple",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "interrogative-particle"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====THAI END====

====TURKISH START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, c, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, v, y, z}.
- Uses mainly CV and CVC syllables; clusters are limited and usually simple.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "agglutinating",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "none",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "extensive",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SOV",
  "adposition_type": "postpositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-final",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "verbal-affix",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "present",
  "question_marking_strategy": "interrogative-particle"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====TURKISH END====

====URDU START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, q, r, s, t, w, y, z}.
- Uses CV and CVC syllables with occasional simple clusters; emphatic/aspirated contrasts use fixed letter sequences.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "fusional",
  "affixation_balance": "suffixing",
  "gender_inventory": "few",
  "number_marking": "obligatory",
  "case_inventory": "moderate",
  "numeral_classifiers": "non-classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SOV",
  "adposition_type": "postpositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-final",
  "relative_clause_marking": "relative-pronoun",
  "alignment_typology": "ergative–absolutive",
  "agreement_pattern": "subject-only",
  "tense_system": "rich",
  "aspect_system": "rich",
  "mood_system": "moderate",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "present",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "wh-fronting"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====URDU END====

====VIETNAMESE START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, y, b, c, d, g, h, k, l, m, n, p, q, r, s, t, v}.
- Words are built from simple syllable blocks; tone is not indicated in spelling.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "isolating",
  "affixation_balance": "prefixing",
  "gender_inventory": "none",
  "number_marking": "optional",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-after-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-after-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-initial",
  "relative_clause_marking": "gap",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "no-agreement",
  "tense_system": "none",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "simple",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "absent",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "interrogative-particle"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====VIETNAMESE END====

====CHINESE START====
You are designing the affix inventory for a synthetic language.

The language uses the following orthography:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
Orthography rules (global):
- All words are lowercase letters a–z only.
- No punctuation, digits, diacritics, or other symbols.
- No capital letters.

Word shape:
- Content words: at least 4 letters.
- Function words (particles, case markers, etc.): 1–3 letters.

Anti-English:
- Do NOT use real English words (e.g. "the", "and", "is", "in", "to").
- Avoid common English morphemes: "ing", "ed", "tion", "sion", "able",
  "less", "ful", "pre", "re", "un", "dis".
- If a word looks close to an English word, change at least 2 letters.
• Language-specific orthography:
- Letter inventory: {a, e, i, o, u, b, c, d, f, g, h, j, k, l, m, n, p, q, r, s, t, w, x, y}.
- Words are made from one or more syllable-like blocks with an initial and a final; tone is not marked.

=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It has the following typological profile:

=== FEATURE VECTOR START ===
{
  "morphological_fusion": "isolating",
  "affixation_balance": "prefixing",
  "gender_inventory": "none",
  "number_marking": "optional",
  "case_inventory": "minimal",
  "numeral_classifiers": "classifier",
  "basic_word_order": "SVO",
  "adposition_type": "prepositional",
  "genitive_noun_order": "genitive-before-noun",
  "adjective_noun_order": "adjective-before-noun",
  "relative_clause_order": "head-final",
  "relative_clause_marking": "particle",
  "alignment_typology": "nominative–accusative",
  "agreement_pattern": "no-agreement",
  "tense_system": "none",
  "aspect_system": "simple",
  "mood_system": "simple",
  "negation_strategy": "preverbal-particle",
  "causative_morphology": "absent",
  "passive_morphology": "present",
  "applicative_morphology": "absent",
  "question_marking_strategy": "interrogative-particle"
}
=== FEATURE VECTOR END ===

Your task:
Define a set of derivational and inflectional affixes that are consistent with this typology and orthography.

Rules:
- All affix forms must follow the orthography (lowercase, valid letters, no hyphens).
- Every affix must be a bound morpheme: it cannot appear as a standalone word.
- Do NOT define any full lexical words here; only affixes.
- Make the system reasonably rich but not overly complex:
  - Include inflectional affixes for number, tense/aspect/mood, and any valence changes (causative, passive, applicative) indicated by the feature vector.
  - Include a few derivational affixes if supported by the typology (e.g., verb→noun, noun→adj), but keep them simple.
- You may not have duplicate forms.
- NO FORMS MAY BE SIMILAR TO ENGLISH!

Return JSON ONLY in this structure:

{
  "inflectional": {
    "number": [
      {
        "name": "PL",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "plural"
      }
    ],
    "tense_aspect_mood": [
      {
        "name": "PST",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "past"
      }
    ],
    "voice_valence": [
      {
        "name": "PASS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "passive"
      },
      {
        "name": "CAUS",
        "type": "suffix",
        "form": "<affix_form>",
        "function": "causative"
      }
    ]
  },
  "derivational": [
    {
      "name": "AGENT_N",
      "type": "suffix",
      "form": "<affix_form>",
      "from_pos": "verb",
      "to_pos": "noun",
      "function": "agentive (one who does X)"
    }
  ]
}

You may leave some lists empty if the feature vector does not support that category (e.g., no voice_valence if there is no passive/causative), but always include the same top-level keys.

Output ONLY the JSON.
====CHINESE END====
